text.title: Terraform Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the Terraform methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: providers + terraform versions MUST be pinned (= operator) in versions.tf. Major-version bumps go via PR with diff + plan attached.
rule.rationale: Unpinned providers cause silent behaviour drift between runs; pinning makes plans reproducible.

rule.statement: State MUST live in a remote backend with locking (S3 + DynamoDB, TF Cloud, GCS + Cloud Storage lock). Local state forbidden for shared environments.
rule.rationale: Local state corrupts on concurrent edits; locking prevents two engineers applying simultaneously.

rule.statement: Apply MUST run only via CI after a reviewed PR with plan attached. No terraform apply from engineer laptops in shared envs.
rule.rationale: Laptop applies skip review, leave no audit trail, and routinely diverge from main.

rule.statement: Repeated patterns (VPC, ECS service, RDS) MUST be modules with semver versioning, sourced from a private registry or git tag. Inline copy-paste of 200+ HCL lines is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Copy-paste forks drift; modules let one fix propagate to N consumers.

rule.statement: Sensitive values MUST come from external sources (Vault / Secrets Manager / SOPS) and be marked sensitive = true. Plaintext secrets in .tfvars or state are forbidden.
rule.rationale: State files leak via S3 misconfig, backups, console access; secrets in state are public secrets.

rule.statement: A weekly CI job MUST run terraform plan against every environment and surface drift (non-zero diff with no PR open). Drift triggers a triage ticket.
rule.rationale: Drift accumulates silently — manual console fixes, vendor automation, expired resources. Weekly plan surfaces drift before disaster.
